skills_i_can_now_do: Tell apart a fact, a bare opinion, and an arguable claim

Diagnose a weak draft that only states a fact and rewrite it into an arguable claim
Use the 3-question checklist (Side? Would people disagree? Reason?) to test a claim
Write my own arguable claim on a new topic without a frame, cold
Know when a prompt wants 'argue' (take a side) vs. 'explain' (no side)
open_questions: How do I actually back up an arguable claim with evidence? I want this skill combined with something new next, not repeated on its own.

skills_i_can_now_do: Add the 'so-what'/stakes move to a claim — explain why anyone should care
Make a claim specific by naming an exact amount or kind

Spot the 'circular stakes' trap — a sentence that uses words like 'matters' without giving actual stakes
Diagnose a fact-report draft that isn't a claim and know it needs a side plus stakes
Run the updated 3-question check (Specific side? Reason? So-what?) on a draft claim
Write a sharp claim with stakes on the same topic without a frame
Transfer the specific-side + reason + stakes move to a brand-new topic (free transit) cold

Transfer the attribution move (tag + purposeful method choice + verify in source) to a brand-new topic cold

side-by-side trap (claim and fact with no hinge)
fake-because (connector word present but clause restates the claim — circular)
